methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $22M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $29.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

data.narrative:
Darkhive is a Series A-stage defense and security company that has raised $22M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms in the same sector and stage, including Scale AI, Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $22M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $44M. Peer-median analysis examines four same-sector, same-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $150M (Ares Industries) to $29.0B (Scale AI), producing a median of $400M. The wide range reflects heterogeneity in the peer set; the median is notably influenced by Scale AI's outlier valuation.

The triangulated analysis produces a low estimate of $37M, a median of $222M, and a high of $460M. The median of $222M sits materially above the stage-progression output but well below the peer median, reflecting tension between early-stage financing metrics and comparable company multiples. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the peer median given Scale AI's substantial valuation premium and whether Darkhive's revenue, growth trajectory, or defensibility metrics justify positioning within this range. Additional data on revenue, customer concentration, and technical differentiation would sharpen this assessment.
